courses.title: Principles of Statistics
content_summary: Introduces descriptive statistics: data classification, tabulation, frequency distributions, and graphical presentation (histograms, bar charts, ogives)
Covers measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode, quantiles) and dispersion (range, standard deviation, coefficient of variation)
Introduces moments, skewness, kurtosis, and shape characteristics of distributions
Covers association of attributes and simple bivariate analysis: correlation and regression via least squares
main_texts: Ross, S. M. (2010). Introductory Statistics (3rd ed.). Academic Press.

courses.title: Principles of Statistics Lab
content_summary: Hands-on computation of descriptive statistics: central tendency, dispersion, skewness, kurtosis using manual calculation
Practical exercises on data tabulation, frequency distribution construction, and graphical representation
Applied correlation and simple regression calculations with data interpretation
main_texts: Ross, S. M. (2010). Introductory Statistics (3rd ed.). Academic Press.

courses.title: Basic Algebra
content_summary: Covers set theory, real number system, and complex numbers including De Moivre's theorem
Summation of trigonometric series and methods such as C+iS
Inequalities (Weierstrass, Cauchy, Chebyshev, Jensen) and polynomial theory including roots, transformations, and Descartes' rule
main_texts: Hall, H. S., & Knight, S. R. (2019). Higher Algebra. Arihant Publications India Limited.

courses.title: Calculus
content_summary: Differential calculus: limits, continuity, higher derivatives, Leibnitz's theorem, Rolle's and Mean Value theorems, Taylor and Maclaurin series
Maxima/minima of single and multivariable functions, partial and total derivatives
Integral calculus: indefinite and definite integrals, Riemann integrals, double and triple integrals, with applications to areas and volumes
main_texts: Anton, H. (1995). Calculus with Analytic Geometry (5th ed.). John Wiley and Sons.

courses.title: English Language
content_summary: Develops reading skills: strategies like critical reading, annotation, summary writing, and contextual vocabulary
Develops writing skills: grammar rules, tense, sentence construction, paragraph development, and use of connectors
main_texts: Soars, L., & Soars, J. (2014). New Headway Upper Intermediate Student's Book. Oxford University Press.

courses.title: Presentation & Viva Voce
content_summary: Oral examination and presentation covering core course concepts of the semester
Develops verbal communication, confidence, and ability to discuss statistical findings with expert audiences

courses.title: Introduction to Probability
content_summary: Covers set theory basics, sample spaces, events, classical and axiomatic probability, conditional probability, and independence
Covers addition and multiplication laws, total probability theorem, Bayes' theorem
Introduces random variables (discrete and continuous), probability and distribution functions, joint/marginal/conditional distributions
Mathematical expectation, variance, covariance, MGF, characteristic functions, and Chebyshev/Markov inequalities
main_texts: Ross, S. M. (2018). A First Course in Probability (9th ed.). Academic Press.
Khan, M. S. H. (2022). Elements of Probability Theory & its Applications. The University Press Limited.

courses.title: Sampling Techniques – I
content_summary: Introduces sampling concepts: probability vs non-probability sampling, census vs survey, sampling and non-sampling errors
Covers Simple Random Sampling (SRS), Stratified, Systematic, and Cluster sampling with estimation of population mean, total, proportion and their standard errors
Ratio and regression methods of estimation; comparison of efficiency across designs
Brief coverage of non-probability methods: convenience, quota, snowball sampling
main_texts: Cochran, W. G. (1977). Sampling Techniques (3rd ed.). John Wiley.
Lohr, S. L. (2010). Sampling: Design and Analysis (2nd ed.). MPS Limited.

courses.title: Sampling Techniques – I Lab
content_summary: Practical drawing of samples using random numbers with and without replacement
Manual computation of unbiased estimators of population mean, total, proportion, and confidence intervals under SRS, stratified, systematic, and cluster sampling
Exercises on ratio and regression estimators, sample size determination, and efficiency comparison
main_texts: Cochran, W. G. (1977). Sampling Techniques (3rd ed.). John Wiley.
Lohr, S. L. (2010). Sampling: Design and Analysis (2nd ed.). MPS Limited.

courses.title: Introduction to Computer and SPSS Lab
content_summary: Basic computer operations, MS Word, Excel (statistical analysis, charts), and PowerPoint
SPSS: data entry, cleaning, management, graphical representation
Statistical analysis in SPSS: central tendency, dispersion, skewness, kurtosis, correlation, and simple regression
main_texts: Field, A. (2018). Discovering Statistics Using IBM SPSS Statistics (5th ed.). SAGE Publications.
George, D., & Mallery, P. (2016). IBM SPSS Statistics 23 Step by Step (14th ed.). Routledge.

courses.title: Introduction to Financial Mathematics
content_summary: Interest rate measurement: simple, compound, nominal, effective rates, force of interest, present and future values
Valuation of annuities (immediate, due, perpetuity), loan repayment and amortization, bond valuation and yield rates
Investment return measures: duration, convexity, immunization, spot and forward rates
Risk management basics, Basel regulations, stock market structure, and introductory accounting principles
main_texts: Broverman, S. A. (2024). Mathematics of Investment and Credit (8th ed.). Actex Publications.

courses.title: Linear Algebra
content_summary: Matrix operations, types of matrices, inverse, adjoint, Cramer's rule, row echelon forms, and rank
Systems of linear equations (homogeneous and non-homogeneous), g-inverse, quadratic forms
Vector spaces, linear dependence/independence, basis, dimension, linear transformations, kernel, rank
Eigenvalues, eigenvectors, Cayley-Hamilton theorem, orthogonalization (Gram-Schmidt), Kronecker products
main_texts: Hadley, G. (1961). Linear Algebra. Addison-Wesley.
Searle, S. R. Matrix Algebra Useful for Statistics. John Wiley.

courses.title: Academic Writing
content_summary: Covers academic writing style: formality, objectivity, coherence, hedging, and paragraph structure
Essay types (argumentative, persuasive, expository), essay development, and formal letter writing
Report writing, in-text citation, and referencing formats (APA, Harvard, Vancouver)
main_texts: Oshima, A., & Hogue, A. (2007). Introduction to Academic Writing (3rd ed.). Longman.
Zemach, D. E., & Rumisek, L. A. (2003). Academic Writing: From Paragraph to Essay. Macmillan.

courses.title: Presentation & Viva Voce
content_summary: Oral examination and presentation covering core course concepts of the semester
Develops verbal communication, confidence, and ability to discuss statistical findings with expert audiences

courses.title: Probability Distributions
content_summary: Review of probability functions, MGF, CGF, characteristic and probability generating functions
Detailed study of discrete distributions: Bernoulli, Binomial, Poisson, Geometric, Hypergeometric, Negative Binomial, Multinomial; brief coverage of Logarithmic, Beta Binomial, Power Series
Detailed study of continuous distributions: Uniform, Exponential, Weibull, Gamma, Beta, Normal, Log-Normal, Cauchy; brief coverage of Logistic, Pareto, Laplace, Gumbel etc.
Truncated and censored distributions; elementary bivariate distributions (Binomial, Poisson, Normal, Gamma, Beta)
main_texts: Krishnamoorthy, K. (2006). Handbook of Statistical Distributions with Applications. Chapman and Hall/CRC.
Johnson, N., Kotz, S., & Kemp, A. (2008). Univariate Discrete Distributions (3rd ed.). John Wiley and Sons.
Johnson, N., & Balakrishnan, N. (1995). Continuous Univariate Distributions (2nd ed.). John Wiley and Sons.

courses.title: Theory of Statistics
content_summary: Sampling distributions from normal and non-normal populations; central limit theorem
Detailed study of chi-square, Student's t, and F distributions; distribution of correlation and regression coefficients
Hypothesis testing: errors, critical regions, tests for mean, variance, proportion, correlation; Bartlett's test, chi-square tests for contingency tables
Order statistics: properties, joint/marginal distributions, distribution of median and range
main_texts: Mood, A., Graybill, F., & Boes, D. (1974). Introduction to the Theory of Statistics (3rd ed.). McGraw-Hill.
Hogg, R. V., McKean, J., & Craig, A. T. (2012). Introduction to Mathematical Statistics (7th ed.). Pearson.

courses.title: Theory of Statistics Lab
content_summary: Practical hypothesis testing for one-sample and two-sample cases: mean, variance, proportion, correlation
Bartlett's homogeneity test, chi-square tests for 2x2 and r×c contingency tables
Sampling from normal and non-normal populations; interpretation of test results
main_texts: Mood, A., Graybill, F., & Boes, D. (1974). Introduction to the Theory of Statistics (3rd ed.). McGraw-Hill.
Hogg, R. V., McKean, J., & Craig, A. T. (2012). Introduction to Mathematical Statistics (7th ed.). Pearson.

courses.title: Statistical Computing: C++ & STATA Lab
content_summary: C++ basics: variables, data types, control structures, functions, arrays, pointers, dynamic memory, classes, inheritance, and polymorphism
STATA: data entry, cleaning, management, graphical representation of data
Statistical analysis in STATA: descriptive statistics, correlation, hypothesis testing, and linear regression with diagnostics
main_texts: Lippman, S., Lajoie, L., & Moo, B. (2013). C++ Primer (5th ed.). Addison-Wesley.
Stroustrup, B. (2013). The C++ Programming Language (4th ed.). Addison-Wesley.
Hamilton, L. C. (2012). Statistics with Stata (12th ed.). Cengage Learning.

courses.title: Advanced Calculus & Differential Equations
content_summary: Improper integrals, Gamma and Beta functions, multivariable functions, Taylor expansion, maxima/minima with Lagrange multipliers
Multiple integrals, Jacobians, Dirichlet integrals, Laplace and inverse Laplace transforms, Fourier series
Ordinary differential equations: first and higher order, homogeneous and non-homogeneous, linear and non-linear, systems of ODEs
main_texts: Anton, H., Bivens, I., & Davis, S. (2012). Calculus Early Transcendentals (10th ed.). John Wiley & Sons.

courses.title: Principles of Microeconomics
content_summary: Basic economic concepts: scarcity, opportunity cost, PPF, demand and supply laws, elasticity, market equilibrium
Consumer behavior: utility theory, indifference curves, budget constraints, income and substitution effects
Production and cost theory: production functions, returns to scale, short-run and long-run costs
Market structures: perfect competition, monopoly, oligopoly, monopolistic competition, pricing decisions
main_texts: Mankiw, N. G. (2022). Principles of Microeconomics (9th ed.). Cengage Learning.
Case, K. E., Fair, R. C., & Oster, S. E. (2019). Principles of Economics (13th ed.). Pearson.

courses.title: Presentation & Viva Voce
content_summary: Oral examination and presentation covering core course concepts of the semester
Develops verbal communication, confidence, and ability to discuss statistical findings with expert audiences

courses.title: Regression Analysis – I
content_summary: Correlation analysis: Pearson, rank, biserial, polychoric, intra-class correlations; spurious and non-sense correlation
Simple linear regression: OLS estimation, Gauss-Markov theorem, hypothesis testing, error variance estimation
Multiple linear regression: three-variable model, separation of effects, multiple and partial correlation, polynomial regression
Residual analysis: graphical methods, lack-of-fit tests, model adequacy checking
main_texts: Montgomery, D. C., Peck, E. A., & Vining, G. G. (2012). Introduction to Linear Regression Analysis (5th ed.). John Wiley.
Gujarati, D. N. (2009). Basic Econometrics. Tata McGraw-Hill Education.
Draper, N. R., & Smith, H. (1998). Applied Regression Analysis (3rd ed.). John Wiley.

courses.title: Regression Analysis – I Lab
content_summary: Software-based computation of correlation coefficients (Pearson, rank, biserial, polychoric, intra-class)
Fitting simple and multiple linear regression models using OLS; testing parameters and checking model adequacy
Residual analysis and model diagnostics using statistical software
main_texts: Montgomery, D. C., Peck, E. A., & Vining, G. G. (2012). Introduction to Linear Regression Analysis (5th ed.). John Wiley.
Gujarati, D. N. (2009). Basic Econometrics. Tata McGraw-Hill Education.
Draper, N. R., & Smith, H. (1998). Applied Regression Analysis (3rd ed.). John Wiley.

courses.title: Numerical Methods
content_summary: Interpolation and extrapolation: Newton's forward/backward, Lagrange's, divided difference, and central difference (Stirling, Bessel) formulas
Root finding methods: Bisection, Newton-Raphson, Iteration; inverse interpolation
Numerical differentiation and integration: Trapezoidal rule, Simpson's 1/3 and 3/8 rules, Weddle's rule
Solving simultaneous linear equations by iterative methods and successive elimination
main_texts: Scarborough, J. B. (2017). Numerical Mathematical Analysis (6th ed.). Oxford & IBH.

courses.title: Real Analysis
content_summary: Real number system: completeness, supremum/infimum, countable and bounded sets
Sequences: convergence, monotone sequences, Bolzano-Weierstrass theorem, Cauchy sequences
Infinite series: convergence tests, absolute and conditional convergence, power series
Limits, continuity, differentiability (Rolle's, MVT, Taylor's theorem), and Riemann integrals
main_texts: Bartle, R. G., & Sherbert, D. R. (2011). Introduction to Real Analysis (4th ed.). John Wiley & Sons.
Rudin, W. (1976). Principles of Mathematical Analysis (3rd ed.). McGraw-Hill.

courses.title: Principles of Macroeconomics
content_summary: Macroeconomic indicators: GDP, GNI, national income measurement methods, real vs nominal GDP, business cycles
Aggregate demand and supply, Keynesian vs Classical views, equilibrium output and multiplier effects
Inflation, unemployment, Phillips curve, money supply and demand, commercial banking and money creation
Monetary policy (central banking) and fiscal policy (taxation, government expenditure, budget)
main_texts: Mankiw, N. G. (2022). Principles of Economics (11th ed.). Cengage Learning.
Case, K. E., Fair, R. C., & Oster, S. E. (2019). Principles of Economics (13th ed.). Pearson.

courses.title: Database Management & Programming
content_summary: Database concepts: DBMS, RDBMS, entity-relationship model, relational model, SQL basics
Database design: indexing, sorting, integrity constraints, transactions, database security and concurrency
Database programming: variables, arrays, functions, procedures, and basic program logic for data operations
main_texts: Silberschatz, A., Korth, H. F., & Sudarshan, S. (2010). Database System Concepts (6th ed.). McGraw-Hill.
Elmasri, R., & Navathe, S. B. (2009). Fundamentals of Database Systems (5th ed.). Pearson Education.

courses.title: Database Management & Programming Lab
content_summary: Practical: creating, modifying, indexing, and sorting databases using software
Designing customer screens, reports, and menus; writing basic database programs
Hands-on SQL queries and database management tasks
main_texts: Silberschatz, A., Korth, H. F., & Sudarshan, S. (2010). Database System Concepts (6th ed.). McGraw-Hill.
Elmasri, R., & Navathe, S. B. (2009). Fundamentals of Database Systems (5th ed.). Pearson Education.

courses.title: Presentation & Viva Voce
content_summary: Oral examination and presentation covering core course concepts of the semester
Develops verbal communication, confidence, and ability to discuss statistical findings with expert audiences

courses.title: Design & Analysis of Experiments – I
content_summary: ANOVA: one-way, two-way, three-way classification with fixed, random, and mixed effect models; model adequacy checking
Multiple comparison tests: Tukey, Newman-Keuls, Duncan, Dunnett, Bonferroni
Standard experimental designs: CRD, RBD, Latin Square, Graeco-Latin Square, replicated Latin Square; estimation, analysis, and relative efficiency
Introduction to factorial experiments: two and three level designs up to n factors
main_texts: Montgomery, D. C. (2020). Design and Analysis of Experiments (10th ed.). Wiley.
Das, M. N., & Giri, N. C. (2017). Design and Analysis of Experiments (3rd ed.). New Age International.
Sahai, H., & Ageel, M. I. (2000). The Analysis of Variance: Fixed, Random and Mixed Models. Birkhauser.

courses.title: Design & Analysis of Experiments – I Lab
content_summary: Practical ANOVA for one-way and two-way classified data with fixed, random, and mixed models
Analysis of CRD, RBD, and Latin Square designs; handling missing data
Multiple comparison tests and factorial experiment analysis (two and three levels) using software
main_texts: Montgomery, D. C. (2020). Design and Analysis of Experiments (10th ed.). Wiley.
Das, M. N., & Giri, N. C. (2017). Design and Analysis of Experiments (3rd ed.). New Age International.

courses.title: Statistical Inference
content_summary: Point estimation: unbiasedness, sufficiency, consistency, efficiency, Cramer-Rao lower bound, MVB; methods of MLE, moments, least squares, Bayes
Interval estimation: confidence intervals for normal, binomial, Poisson parameters; large sample intervals
Parametric tests: Neyman-Pearson lemma, most powerful tests, UMP tests, critical regions
Non-parametric tests: runs test, goodness-of-fit, sign test, Wilcoxon signed rank, rank-based two-sample and k-sample tests, measures of association
main_texts: Mukhopadhyay, N. (2000). Probability and Statistical Inference. CRC Press.
Rohatgi, V. K. (2013). Statistical Inference. Dover Publications.
Mood, A., Graybill, F., & Boes, D. (1974). Introduction to the Theory of Statistics (3rd ed.). McGraw-Hill.

courses.title: Statistical Inference Lab
content_summary: Drawing samples from univariate and bivariate normal distributions; applying MLE and method of moments
Constructing confidence intervals for normal, binomial, and Poisson parameters
Hypothesis testing for univariate/bivariate normal and discrete distributions; power curves; non-parametric tests
main_texts: Hogg, R. V., & Craig, A. T. Introduction to Mathematical Statistics. Macmillan.
Mood, A., Graybill, F., & Boes, D. (1974). Introduction to the Theory of Statistics (3rd ed.). McGraw-Hill.

courses.title: Statistical Simulation & Data Analysis (R & Python)
content_summary: Simulation concepts: discrete and continuous simulation, random number and random variate generation
Monte Carlo integration; functions including incomplete gamma/beta, chi-square, t, F, binomial, hypergeometric distributions
R and Python: basic operations, data management, loops, functions, summary statistics, graphical procedures, and statistical models with probability distributions
main_texts: Ross, S. M. (2006). Simulation (4th ed.). Academic Press.
Cohen, Y., & Cohen, J. Y. (2008). Statistics and Data with R: An Applied Approach Through Examples. John Wiley & Sons.
VanderPlas, J. (2016). Python Data Science Handbook: Essential Tools for Working with Data. O'Reilly.

courses.title: Statistical Simulation & Data Analysis Lab
content_summary: Practical R and Python: basic operations, data management, data frames, grouping, and graphical representation
Data analysis using R/Python: regression, ANOVA, all-possible regressions, model diagnostics, RBD analysis, split-plot, non-orthogonal designs
main_texts: Ross, S. M. (2006). Simulation (4th ed.). Academic Press.
Cohen, Y., & Cohen, J. Y. (2008). Statistics and Data with R: An Applied Approach Through Examples. John Wiley & Sons.
VanderPlas, J. (2016). Python Data Science Handbook. O'Reilly.

courses.title: Linear Programming
content_summary: LP formulation, graphical method, simplex method, revised simplex, primal-dual problems, sensitivity analysis
Integer programming: cutting plane and branch-and-bound algorithms
Transportation and assignment models; network models: minimal spanning tree, maximal flow, shortest route
Game theory: two-person zero-sum games and their LP equivalence
main_texts: Bazaraa, M. S., Jarvis, J. J., & Sherali, H. D. (2009). Linear Programming and Network Flows (4th ed.). Wiley.
Taha, H. A. (2010). Operations Research: An Introduction (9th ed.). Pearson.

courses.title: Presentation & Viva Voce
content_summary: Oral examination and presentation covering core course concepts of the semester
Develops verbal communication, confidence, and ability to discuss statistical findings with expert audiences

courses.title: Stochastic Processes
content_summary: Modern probability: Borel fields, probability measure, conditional expectation in sigma-algebras
Characteristic and probability generating functions; convergence of random variables, laws of large numbers, CLT, Martingales
Markov chains: transition matrix, classification of states, ergodic properties, stability, gambler's ruin, random walk
Poisson process, birth-death processes, immigration, renewal theory; queuing models: M/M/1, M/M/1/N, M/M/m
main_texts: Ross, S. M. Stochastic Processes (2nd ed.). John Wiley & Sons.
Bhat, B. R. (2007). Modern Probability Theory. New Age International.
Medhi, J. (1994). Stochastic Processes. New Age International.
Sufian, A. J. M. (2017). Stochastic Processes and Their Applications in Business. The University Press Limited.

courses.title: Demography
content_summary: Sources of demographic data: census, surveys, vital registration; types of errors and detection methods
Fertility measures: CBR, GFR, ASFR, TFR, GRR, NRR, Bongaarts' model; mortality measures: CDR, ASDR, infant mortality
Nuptiality measures, life table construction and application, population pyramids, migration measures
Population growth models: exponential, Gompertz, and logistic curve fitting
main_texts: Islam, M. N. (2015). An Introduction to Demographic Techniques. Mullick & Brothers.
Kpedekpo, G. M. K. (1982). Essentials of Demographic Analysis for Africa. Heinemann.

courses.title: Demography Lab
content_summary: Practical computation of demographic rates and ratios (CBR, CDR, TFR, GRR, NRR, SR)
Construction of population pyramids, complete and abridged life tables
Fitting growth curves and estimating population growth rates from real data
main_texts: Islam, M. N. (2015). An Introduction to Demographic Techniques. Mullick & Brothers.
Kpedekpo, G. M. K. (1982). Essentials of Demographic Analysis for Africa. Heinemann.

courses.title: Data Analysis with SAS Lab
content_summary: SAS fundamentals: program structure, syntax, accessing data sets and libraries
Data management: reading, cleaning, merging/concatenating datasets, handling missing data, formatting
Statistical analysis using SAS PROC steps: MEANS, FREQ, CORR, REG, ANOVA, GLM, T-TEST, UNIVARIATE, and SAS graphics
main_texts: Der, G., & Everitt, B. S. (2008). A Handbook of Statistical Analyses Using SAS (3rd ed.). CRC Press.

courses.title: Regression Analysis – II
content_summary: Generalized and weighted least squares; Gauss-Markov-Aitken theorem; estimation and tests for linear restrictions
Heteroscedasticity: detection, testing, and estimation under heteroscedastic disturbances
Multicollinearity: detection, effects, remedial measures, estimable functions, variable selection
Autocorrelation: sources, consequences, tests, and parameter estimation; dummy variables; errors-in-variables models
main_texts: Gujarati, D. N. (2009). Basic Econometrics (3rd ed.). McGraw-Hill.
Johnston, J. (1979). Econometric Methods. McGraw-Hill.
Neter, J., Wasserman, W., & Kutner, M. H. (1983). Applied Linear Regression Models. Richard D. Irwin.

courses.title: Regression Analysis – II Lab
content_summary: Fitting multiple regression models and testing parameters using software
Detection and correction of heteroscedasticity and multicollinearity; autocorrelation tests and remediation
Fitting dummy variable regression models and performing hypothesis tests
main_texts: Gujarati, D. N. (2009). Basic Econometrics (3rd ed.). McGraw-Hill.
Johnston, J. (1979). Econometric Methods. McGraw-Hill.
Neter, J., Wasserman, W., & Kutner, M. H. (1983). Applied Linear Regression Models. Richard D. Irwin.

courses.title: Field Work
content_summary: 7-day field visit to an industry, organization, or institution for hands-on data collection experience
Students apply sampling techniques, collaborate in groups, and submit a written field report

courses.title: Presentation & Viva Voce
content_summary: Oral examination and presentation covering core course concepts of the semester
Develops verbal communication, confidence, and ability to discuss statistical findings with expert audiences

courses.title: Economic Statistics
content_summary: Index numbers: price, quantity, value indices; cost of living index; tests of index numbers
Time series: components, decomposition, trend and seasonality estimation methods
Income distribution: Pareto's law, Lorenz curve, Gini coefficient, lognormal distribution, stochastic income models
Production theory: Cobb-Douglas and CES production functions, cost minimization, profit maximization, input-output analysis (Leontief model), dynamic growth models (Harrod-Domar, Cobweb)
main_texts: Allen, R. G. D. (1970). Mathematical Economics. Macmillan.
Chiang, A. C., & Wainwright, K. (2005). Fundamental Methods of Mathematical Economics (4th ed.). McGraw-Hill.

courses.title: Economic Statistics Lab
content_summary: Computation of price/income elasticity of demand; fitting demand and Engel curves
Fitting Pareto and lognormal distributions; Lorenz curve and Gini ratio estimation
Estimation of Cobb-Douglas, CES, and trans-log production functions; input-output table construction; fitting growth curves
main_texts: Allen, R. G. D. (1970). Mathematical Economics. Macmillan.
Chiang, A. C., & Wainwright, K. (2005). Fundamental Methods of Mathematical Economics (4th ed.). McGraw-Hill.

courses.title: Applied Statistics
content_summary: Statistical quality control: control charts for variables (mean, range, SD) and attributes (p-chart, d-chart, c-chart), modified control limits
Acceptance sampling: OC curves, ASN, ATI, AOQL; single, double, and sequential sampling plans (Dodge-Romig)
Educational/psychological statistics: scaling (Z-scores, T-scores, percentile), test reliability (test-retest, split-half, Kuder-Richardson), validity, and IQ
Published statistics in Bangladesh: sources, classifications, and limitations of official statistical publications
main_texts: Grant, E. L., & Leavenworth, R. S. (2004). Statistical Quality Control (7th ed.). Tata McGraw-Hill.
Guilford, J. P., & Fruchter, B. (1977). Fundamental Statistics in Psychology and Education (6th ed.). McGraw-Hill.

courses.title: Applied Statistics Lab
content_summary: Drawing control charts (mean, range, SD, p, d, c) and computing modified control limits using industrial data
Constructing OC, ASN, ATI, AOQ curves for single, double, and sequential sampling plans
Computing test item difficulty, Z/T/percentile scores, scaling of rankings/ratings, and reliability coefficients for educational data
main_texts: Grant, E. L., & Leavenworth, R. S. (2004). Statistical Quality Control (7th ed.). Tata McGraw-Hill.
Guilford, J. P., & Fruchter, B. (1977). Fundamental Statistics in Psychology and Education (6th ed.). McGraw-Hill.

courses.title: Design & Analysis of Experiments – II
content_summary: Linear estimation, estimable functions, optimality of least squares, tests of hypothesis for non-full-rank models
Incomplete block designs: BIB design construction and analysis (intra- and inter-block), Youden squares, lattice designs, PBIB designs
Confounded factorial experiments: total, partial, simultaneous confounding; fractional replicates; asymmetric factorial designs
Split-plot, split-split-plot, strip-plot, nested designs; analysis of covariance (ANCOVA) with one or more ancillary variables
main_texts: Montgomery, D. C. (2020). Design and Analysis of Experiments (10th ed.). Wiley.
Lawson, J. (2015). Design and Analysis of Experiments with R. Chapman and Hall/CRC.

courses.title: Design & Analysis of Experiments – II Lab
content_summary: Practical analysis of confounded factorial experiments (total, partial, simultaneous) and fractionally replicated designs
Intra- and inter-block analysis of BIBD; analysis of split-plot, split-split-plot, strip-plot, and nested designs
Fitting and interpreting ANCOVA models using statistical software
main_texts: Montgomery, D. C. (2020). Design and Analysis of Experiments (10th ed.). Wiley.
Lawson, J. (2015). Design and Analysis of Experiments with R. Chapman and Hall/CRC.

courses.title: Sampling Techniques – II
content_summary: Sampling with unequal probability: Horvitz-Thompson estimator; Brewer, Durbin, Raj, Murthy, Rao-Hartley-Cochran methods; PPS systematic sampling
Multi-stage sampling: two and three stage with equal/unequal clusters; self-weighting estimates
Double sampling for stratification, ratio, regression, and difference estimation; sampling on two or more occasions
Non-sampling errors: interviewer bias, non-response, interpenetrating subsamples; familiarity with Bangladesh large-scale surveys (DHS, HIES, SVRS)
main_texts: Cochran, W. G. (1977). Sampling Techniques (3rd ed.). John Wiley.
Lohr, S. L. (2010). Sampling: Design and Analysis (2nd ed.). MPS Limited.

courses.title: Sampling Techniques – II Lab
content_summary: Practical implementation of PPS sampling, two-stage sampling (equal and unequal clusters), and double sampling
Computation of population totals, means, and standard errors under complex sampling designs
Self-weighting estimates and interpretation of results
main_texts: Cochran, W. G. (1977). Sampling Techniques (3rd ed.). John Wiley.
Lohr, S. L. (2010). Sampling: Design and Analysis (2nd ed.). MPS Limited.

courses.title: Presentation & Viva Voce
content_summary: Oral examination and presentation covering core course concepts of the semester
Develops verbal communication, confidence, and ability to discuss statistical findings with expert audiences

courses.title: Multivariate Analysis
content_summary: Non-central chi-square, t, and F distributions; distribution of quadratic forms and Cochran's theorem
Multivariate normal distribution: derivation, marginal/conditional distributions, MGF, characteristic function, and properties
Multivariate sampling distributions: Hotelling's T², Mahalanobis D², Wishart distribution; tests for covariance/correlation patterns
Multivariate linear models: multivariate regression, MANOVA, MANCOVA, conditional Gaussian and independence graphs
main_texts: Johnson, R. A., & Wichern, D. W. (2015). Applied Multivariate Statistical Analysis (6th ed.). Pearson Prentice Hall.
Rencher, A. C., & Christensen, W. F. (2012). Methods of Multivariate Analysis (3rd ed.). John Wiley & Sons.

courses.title: Multivariate Analysis Lab
content_summary: Simulation of multivariate normal variates using software
Tests of mean vectors using Hotelling's T² and Mahalanobis D²; tests for covariance and correlation patterns
Estimation of multivariate linear regression and construction of MANOVA tables
main_texts: Johnson, R. A., & Wichern, D. W. (2015). Applied Multivariate Statistical Analysis (6th ed.). Pearson Prentice Hall.

courses.title: Biostatistics & Epidemiology
content_summary: Survival analysis: censoring types, survival/hazard functions, mean residual life; non-parametric methods (Kaplan-Meier, Nelson-Aalen, life table, log-rank, Wilcoxon)
Parametric methods for exponential and Weibull distributions; Cox proportional hazard model (basic concept)
Epidemiology: study designs (case-control, cohort, prospective, retrospective); measures of association (RR, OR, AR, incidence, prevalence, sensitivity, specificity, ROC curve)
Logistic and Poisson regression models, McNemar test, correlated proportions, basic linear mixed models
main_texts: Kleinbaum, D. G., Kupper, L. L., & Morgenstern, H. (1982). Epidemiologic Research: Principles and Quantitative Methods. John Wiley & Sons.
Rothman, K. J. (2012). Epidemiology: An Introduction (2nd ed.). Oxford University Press.
Klein, J. P., & Moeschberger, M. L. (2003). Survival Analysis: Techniques for Censored and Truncated Data (2nd ed.). Springer.

courses.title: Biostatistics & Epidemiology Lab
content_summary: Estimation of survival probabilities using non-parametric methods (complete and censored data)
Fitting parametric survival models (exponential, Weibull); hypothesis testing with complete and censored samples
Computing epidemiological measures (incidence, prevalence, RR, OR, AR); applying logistic regression, McNemar test, and linear mixed models using software
main_texts: Klein, J. P., & Moeschberger, M. L. (1997). Survival Analysis: Techniques for Censored and Truncated Data. Springer.
Lawless, J. F. (2002). Statistical Models and Methods for Lifetime Data (2nd ed.). John Wiley & Sons.
Rothman, K. J. (2012). Epidemiology: An Introduction (2nd ed.). Oxford University Press.

courses.title: Generalized Linear Models
content_summary: Exponential family of distributions (Bernoulli, Binomial, Poisson, Gamma, Normal) and their properties
GLM components: random, systematic, link functions; Poisson regression; MLE via iteratively reweighted least squares (IRLS)
Inference: deviance, scaled deviance, Pearson chi-square, residuals (Pearson, Anscombe); logit link, nominal and ordinal logistic regression; goodness-of-fit (Hosmer-Lemeshow, AIC, BIC)
Quasi-likelihood methods; GEE for repeated measures; linear and generalized linear mixed effects models
main_texts: Dobson, A. J., & Barnett, A. G. (2008). An Introduction to Generalized Linear Models (3rd ed.). Chapman and Hall/CRC.

courses.title: Research Methodology
content_summary: Research concepts: types of research, inductive/deductive approaches, ethical issues, research question formulation
Research designs: exploratory, descriptive (cross-sectional, longitudinal, panel), and causal/experimental studies
Sampling design, questionnaire construction, data collection methods (interview, FGD, IDI, KII), data processing and analysis
Research proposal writing, report structure, referencing, and reliability/validity of measurements
main_texts: Kothari, C. R. (2013). Research Methodology: Methods and Techniques (3rd ed.). New Age International.
Akand, M. A. S. (2019). Research Methodology: A Complete Direction for Learners (2nd ed.). Akanda & Sons.

courses.title: Introduction to Data Science
content_summary: Data science concepts: objectives, process, tools, methodology, ethics, bias, and privacy
Data preprocessing: cleaning, encoding, missing value handling, normalization, feature engineering, exploratory data analysis
Machine learning concepts: supervised (regression, classification: KNN, decision tree, SVM, random forest, naive Bayes, ANN) and unsupervised (clustering, association rules) learning
Big data: characteristics, types, analytics techniques, platforms, and tools; hands-on project with R, Python, and SQL
main_texts: Kalita, J. K., Bhattacharyya, D. K., & Roy, S. (2023). Fundamentals of Data Science: Theory and Practice. Elsevier.
Ou, G., Zhu, Z., Dong, B., & Weinan, E. (2023). Introduction to Data Science. World Scientific.

courses.title: Research Project
content_summary: 30-day independent research project: students select a topic with a supervisor, design the study, collect and analyze data
Written report submitted using LaTeX; evaluated by assigned teacher, external examiner, and oral presentation to examination committee

courses.title: 30 Days Comprehensive (Optional)
content_summary: Comprehensive review course covering foundational and advanced topics across the entire program

Major topic areas: descriptive statistics, probability and distributions, sampling distributions, point and interval estimation, hypothesis testing (parametric and non-parametric), sampling techniques (SRS, stratified, systematic, cluster), and experimental designs (CRD, RBD, Latin Square)
main_texts: Gupta, S. C., & Kapoor, V. K. (2000). Fundamentals of Mathematical Statistics (10th ed.). Sultan Chand and Sons.
Ross, S. M. (2010). Introductory Statistics (3rd ed.). Academic Press.
Ross, S. M. (2018). A First Course in Probability (9th ed.). Academic Press.

courses.title: Presentation & Viva Voce
content_summary: Oral examination and presentation covering core course concepts of the semester
Develops verbal communication, confidence, and ability to discuss statistical findings with expert audiences
